summary:
Acute rheumatic valvulitis, primarily targeting the mitral valve, represents the earliest sign of valvular damage during an episode of acute rheumatic fever. This condition characterizes itself by rapid development followed quickly thereafter by potential progression into chronic conditions such as stenosis if left untreated.\boxed{A}

The summary focuses solely on these two critical aspects for answering the provided multiple-choice query accurately while adhering strictly within specified constraints about length and content relevance. \boxed{A}
